source.title: 2024 AHA/ACC/ACS/ASNC/HRS/SCA/SCCT/SCMR/SVM Guideline for Perioperative Cardiovascular Management for Noncardiac Surgery
source.organization: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

signals: American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association

extraction.summary: This clinical guideline provides recommendations for perioperative cardiovascular evaluation and management of adult patients (≥18 years) undergoing noncardiac surgery. It emphasizes a stepwise approach to cardiac assessment, appropriate use of diagnostic testing, and evidence-based management strategies for cardiovascular conditions during the perioperative period.

population.risk_criteria: Patients undergoing noncardiac surgery with known or at risk of cardiovascular disease
population.disease_definition: Cardiovascular disease or risk factors requiring perioperative evaluation and management
population.severity_staging: Risk stratification based on cardiac risk indices, functional capacity assessment, and surgical risk

recommendations.recommendation: Use stepwise approach to perioperative cardiac assessment to determine when surgery should proceed or when pause for further evaluation is warranted
recommendations.notes: Assists clinicians in decision making

recommendations.recommendation: Cardiovascular screening and treatment should adhere to same indications as nonsurgical patients, timed to avoid delays in surgery and chosen to avoid overscreening and overtreatment
recommendations.notes: Avoid unnecessary testing

recommendations.recommendation: Perform stress testing judiciously, especially in lower risk patients, and only when testing would be appropriate independent of planned surgery
recommendations.notes: Avoid unnecessary stress testing

recommendations.recommendation: Emphasize team-based care when managing patients with complex anatomy or unstable cardiovascular disease
recommendations.notes: Multidisciplinary approach

recommendations.recommendation: Discontinue SGLT2 inhibitors 3-4 days before surgery to minimize risk of perioperative ketoacidosis
recommendations.notes: New therapy with perioperative implications

recommendations.recommendation: Follow patients with newly diagnosed perioperative atrial fibrillation closely after surgery to treat reversible causes and assess need for rhythm control and anticoagulation
recommendations.notes: Increased stroke risk

recommendations.recommendation: Use perioperative anticoagulant bridging selectively only in highest risk patients for thrombotic complications, not recommended in majority of cases
recommendations.notes: Selective use only

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with known CVD being considered for NCS, a validated risk-prediction tool can be useful to estimate the risk of perioperative MACE
recommendations.notes: Multiple risk indices available including RCRI, NSQIP MICA calculator, universal NSQIP surgical risk calculator; significant variability exists between different tools

recommendations.recommendation: Use structured functional capacity assessment (DASI) in elevated-risk noncardiac surgery patients
recommendations.notes: To stratify risk of perioperative adverse cardiovascular events

recommendations.recommendation: Perform frailty assessment in patients ≥65 years and those <64 years with perceived frailty undergoing elevated-risk surgery
recommendations.notes: Using validated tools for evaluating perioperative risk

recommendations.recommendation: Measure BNP or NT-proBNP in patients with known CVD, age ≥65, or age ≥45 with CVD symptoms undergoing elevated-risk surgery
recommendations.notes: To supplement perioperative risk evaluation

recommendations.recommendation: Consider measuring cardiac troponin preoperatively in high-risk patients
recommendations.notes: May supplement risk evaluation but no specific management changes proven

recommendations.recommendation: Obtain preoperative 12-lead ECG in patients with known CVD or CVD symptoms undergoing elevated-risk surgery
recommendations.notes: To establish baseline and guide perioperative management

recommendations.recommendation: Perform preoperative ECG in patients ≥65 years or with known cardiovascular disease for baseline comparison
recommendations.notes: Comparing with previous tracings helpful when abnormalities identified

recommendations.recommendation: Perform preoperative LV function assessment in patients with new dyspnea, HF findings, or suspected ventricular dysfunction
recommendations.notes: Helps guide perioperative management

recommendations.recommendation: Do not perform routine preoperative ECG in low-risk patients
recommendations.notes: Little effect on treatment or complication rates

recommendations.recommendation: Do not perform routine LV function assessment in asymptomatic, clinically stable patients
recommendations.notes: Lack of demonstrated benefit

recommendations.recommendation: Consider stress testing in elevated-risk patients with poor/unknown functional capacity and elevated cardiovascular risk
recommendations.notes: May be considered to evaluate for inducible myocardial ischemia

recommendations.recommendation: Do not perform routine stress testing in low-risk patients, those with adequate functional capacity, or undergoing low-risk procedures
recommendations.notes: Costly, may delay surgery, no demonstrated clinical benefit

recommendations.recommendation: Consider CCTA in elevated-risk patients with poor/unknown functional capacity for detection of high-risk coronary anatomy
recommendations.notes: May be considered in select cases

recommendations.recommendation: CCTA may be considered in select cases if high-risk coronary anatomy is suspected in patients with elevated risk and low/indeterminate functional capacity
recommendations.notes: Only if guideline-concordant indication exists independent of planned surgery

recommendations.recommendation: Routine preoperative invasive coronary angiography is not recommended to improve perioperative outcomes
recommendations.notes: No benefit class recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Coronary revascularization and deferral of surgery is recommended in patients with ACS
recommendations.notes: To reduce perioperative cardiovascular events

recommendations.recommendation: Coronary revascularization is reasonable in patients with hemodynamically significant left main stenosis ≥50%
recommendations.notes: For elective NCS planning

recommendations.recommendation: Routine preoperative coronary revascularization is not recommended for non-left main CAD
recommendations.notes: No benefit class recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Continue medical therapy for hypertension throughout perioperative period in most patients
recommendations.notes: Caution in low BP, elderly ≥65 years, high hypotension risk

recommendations.recommendation: Maintain intraoperative MAP ≥60-65 mmHg or SBP ≥90 mmHg
recommendations.notes: To reduce risk of myocardial injury

recommendations.recommendation: Treat postoperative hypotension (MAP <60-65 or SBP <90 mmHg)
recommendations.notes: To limit cardiovascular, cerebrovascular, renal events and mortality

recommendations.recommendation: Withhold SGLT2 inhibitors for 3-4 days before surgery in HF patients
recommendations.notes: To reduce risk of perioperative metabolic acidosis

recommendations.recommendation: Continue GDMT for compensated HF (excluding SGLT2i) in perioperative period
recommendations.notes: Unless contraindicated, to reduce risk of worsening HF

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with HF undergoing elective NCS, SGLT2i should be withheld for 3-4 days before surgery when feasible
recommendations.notes: Canagliflozin, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin should be stopped ≥3 days and ertugliflozin ≥4 days before scheduled surgery

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with compensated HF undergoing NCS, continue GDMT (excluding SGLT2i) in perioperative period unless contraindicated
recommendations.notes: To reduce risk of worsening heart failure

recommendations.recommendation: For patients with HCM undergoing NCS, factors that aggravate or trigger dynamic outflow obstruction should be avoided
recommendations.notes: Avoid positive inotropic agents, tachycardia, reduced preload

recommendations.recommendation: In patients receiving stable doses of targeted medical therapies for PAH undergoing NCS, continue these agents
recommendations.notes: To reduce risk for development of perioperative MACE

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with severe PH undergoing elevated-risk NCS, referral to specialized PH center is reasonable
recommendations.notes: For risk assessment, optimization, and postoperative management

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with intermediate-to elevated-risk CHD lesions undergoing elective NCS, preoperative consultation with ACHD specialist is recommended
recommendations.notes: Before surgery

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with severe AS should be evaluated for aortic valve intervention before elective NCS to reduce perioperative risk
recommendations.notes: Strong recommendation with limited data

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with suspected moderate or severe AS undergoing elevated-risk NCS, preoperative echocardiography is recommended before elective NCS
recommendations.notes: Expert opinion based

recommendations.recommendation: Coordination with LVAD care team on appropriate timing and perioperative considerations of elective NCS is recommended
recommendations.notes: To mitigate risk of perioperative MACE

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with severe mitral stenosis should be evaluated for mitral valve intervention before elective NCS
recommendations.notes: Non-randomized evidence

recommendations.recommendation: Preoperative echocardiography is recommended in patients with suspected moderate or severe valvular regurgitation before elective NCS
recommendations.notes: To guide perioperative management

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with indications for mitral/aortic valve surgery should have intervention before NCS when feasible
recommendations.notes: Based on 2020 ACC/AHA VHD guideline

recommendations.recommendation: TAVI patients can reasonably undergo NCS early (within 30 days) if clinically indicated
recommendations.notes: Evidence supports safety within 30 days of successful TAVI

recommendations.recommendation: Treat underlying triggers contributing to rapid AF in NCS setting
recommendations.notes: Address sepsis, anemia, pain, electrolyte imbalances

recommendations.recommendation: CIED patients having elective NCS should have management plan developed before surgery if EMI anticipated
recommendations.notes: Must identify device type, manufacturer, model

recommendations.recommendation: Delay elective NCS for ≥3 months after stroke/TIA to reduce recurrence risk
recommendations.notes: Risk reduction diminishes over time but remains elevated

recommendations.recommendation: OSA screening using validated questionnaires is reasonable to assess perioperative complication risk
recommendations.notes: Associated with increased MI, AF, HF risk

recommendations.recommendation: Continue statin therapy in patients currently on statins scheduled for NCS
recommendations.notes: Reduces risk of MACE

recommendations.recommendation: Initiate statin in statin-naïve patients who meet criteria based on ASCVD history or 10-year risk
recommendations.notes: With intention of long-term use

recommendations.recommendation: Consider omitting RAAS inhibitors 24 hours before elevated-risk surgery in select patients
recommendations.notes: To limit intraoperative hypotension in patients with controlled BP

recommendations.recommendation: Continue RAAS inhibitors in patients with HFrEF
recommendations.notes: Perioperative continuation is reasonable

recommendations.recommendation: Do not initiate low-dose clonidine perioperatively
recommendations.notes: Does not reduce cardiovascular risk and may increase hypotension/bradycardia

recommendations.recommendation: Multidisciplinary team should determine antiplatelet management and surgery timing
recommendations.notes: Balance bleeding, thrombosis, and delayed surgery risks

recommendations.recommendation: Continue aspirin (75-100mg) if possible in patients with prior PCI
recommendations.notes: To reduce cardiac events

recommendations.recommendation: Multidisciplinary team approach for antiplatelet/anticoagulation decisions
recommendations.notes: Should involve patient, family, cardiologist, surgeon, anesthesiologist

recommendations.recommendation: Continue aspirin monotherapy in patients with prior PCI when DAPT must be interrupted
recommendations.notes: Whenever surgically feasible

recommendations.recommendation: Time-based OAC interruption using standardized protocols
recommendations.notes: Balance thrombotic versus bleeding risk

recommendations.recommendation: Routine perioperative bridging is not recommended for most patients
recommendations.notes: Due to increased bleeding risk without thrombotic benefit

recommendations.recommendation: Preoperative bridging with heparin for high thrombotic risk VKA patients
recommendations.notes: Only in select very high-risk patients

recommendations.recommendation: Most patients with AF will not benefit from bridging anticoagulation during perioperative period
recommendations.notes: Based on BRIDGE trial showing no benefit but increased bleeding risk

recommendations.recommendation: Continue chronic beta blockers through perioperative period
recommendations.notes: Acute discontinuation is harmful

recommendations.recommendation: Do not initiate beta blockers on day of surgery
recommendations.notes: Increased risk of postoperative mortality

recommendations.recommendation: Discontinue SGLT2 inhibitors 3-4 days before surgery
recommendations.notes: To reduce risk of perioperative metabolic acidosis

recommendations.recommendation: Continue metformin during perioperative period
recommendations.notes: Reasonable to maintain glycemic control

recommendations.recommendation: Use TEE or FoCUS for unexplained hemodynamic instability
recommendations.notes: If expertise readily available

recommendations.recommendation: Do not use routine intraoperative TEE without risk factors
recommendations.notes: Not recommended for screening or ischemia monitoring

recommendations.recommendation: Measure cTn at 24 and 48 hours after elevated-risk NCS in patients with known CVD, CVD symptoms, or age ≥65 with risk factors
recommendations.notes: For MINS identification

recommendations.recommendation: Do not routinely screen with cTn levels after low-risk NCS without signs/symptoms
recommendations.notes: Low likelihood of benefit in low-risk populations

recommendations.recommendation: Use TEE for unexplained severe hemodynamic instability that persists despite corrective therapy
recommendations.notes: When available and appropriately trained practitioners

recommendations.recommendation: Maintain normothermia in patients with CVD undergoing NCS
recommendations.notes: To avoid perioperative complications

recommendations.recommendation: Consider outpatient follow-up for cardiovascular risk factor optimization in patients who develop MINS
recommendations.notes: Especially those not previously known to have excess cardiovascular risk

recommendations.recommendation: Use tranexamic acid in NCS with expected blood loss
recommendations.notes: To reduce intraoperative blood loss, reduce transfusions, avoid anemia

recommendations.recommendation: Administer preoperative iron therapy (oral or IV) in iron deficiency anemia before elective NCS
recommendations.notes: To reduce blood transfusions and increase hemoglobin

medications.indication: Diabetes management
Heart failure patients undergoing surgery
Heart failure patients undergoing surgery
Heart failure GDMT
Heart failure GDMT, HCM management
Precapillary PH with elevated-risk NCS
HCM patients with hypotension
Heart rate control in mitral stenosis patients
Heart rate control in mitral stenosis patients
Heart rate control in mitral stenosis patients
Heart rate control in mitral stenosis patients
Continue perioperatively in TAVI patients to reduce thrombotic risk
Up to 6 months after MV TEER in sinus rhythm patients

medications.drug: Beta blockers or calcium channel blockers
medications.indication: AV nodal blockade for AF rate control
Perioperative cardiovascular risk reduction
NOT RECOMMENDED - increases hypotension/bradycardia risk
DAPT component - interrupt 5-7 days before surgery
Bridging in very high thrombotic risk patients
Dabigatran reversal for urgent surgery
Factor Xa inhibitor reversal for urgent surgery
Perioperative glycemic control
Reduce intraoperative bleeding in NCS with expected blood loss

medications.dose: IV: ferric carboxymaltose, ferric derisomaltose, ferumoxytol (higher single doses); iron sucrose (200-300mg per infusion)
medications.indication: Preoperative treatment of iron deficiency anemia
Antithrombotic therapy for MINS (may be considered)

red_flags: Myocardial injury after noncardiac surgery - should not be ignored as it portends real consequences
Unexplained hemodynamic instability requiring emergency focused cardiac ultrasound
Newly diagnosed atrial fibrillation with increased stroke risk
Unstable cardiovascular disease requiring team-based care
Emergency surgery with immediate threat to life or limb
Urgent surgery with threat to life or limb within 24 hours
Mobitz type II or higher atrioventricular block
Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
New-onset atrial fibrillation
Prolonged QT interval
ST-segment elevation
Significant pathologic Q-waves
Significant Q-waves on ECG
LV hypertrophy
ST depression
T-wave inversion
Mobitz type II or higher block
Bundle branch blocks
Atrial fibrillation
QT interval prolongation
New dyspnea with physical examination findings of heart failure
Worsening ventricular dysfunction symptoms
Severely reduced LVEF ≤30%
Grade 3 diastolic dysfunction
Acute coronary syndromes (STEMI, high-risk NSTEMI, unstable angina)
Cardiogenic shock
ACS with refractory angina, intractable arrhythmias, or hemodynamic instability
GRACE score >140
Hemodynamically significant left main coronary stenosis ≥50%
Uncontrolled hypertension with systolic ≥180 mmHg or diastolic ≥110 mmHg in elevated-risk surgery patients with cardiovascular risk factors
Intraoperative hypotension with MAP <60-65 mmHg or SBP <90 mmHg for >15 minutes
Postoperative hypotension with MAP <60-65 mmHg or SBP <90 mmHg
Dynamic LVOT obstruction in HCM patients
Acute decompensated heart failure
Hemodynamic instability in PH patients
Metabolic acidosis from SGLT2i
Euglycemic ketoacidosis
Severe RV dysfunction with PH
Cyanotic congenital heart disease with decompensation
Cyanosis with right-to-left shunts requiring paradoxical embolism prevention
Hemodynamic instability in severe aortic stenosis
Symptomatic severe aortic stenosis
Pulmonary hypertension with elevated PA pressures
Heart failure symptoms
Arrhythmias requiring treatment
LVAD complications including pump thrombosis
Acute rejection in heart transplant recipients
Hemodynamically unstable AF with rapid ventricular response requiring synchronized cardioversion
Inappropriate ICD shocks due to EMI during surgery
Pacemaker inhibition in pacemaker-dependent patients during EMI exposure
New neurological deficits suggesting perioperative stroke
Severe AR with hypotension, arrhythmias, or heart failure decompensation
NCS within 30 days of BMS or <3 months of DES-PCI requiring antiplatelet interruption
Uncontrolled hypertension (SBP >160 mmHg)
Severe hypotension (SBP <80 mmHg)
Clinically significant bradycardia or cardiac arrest with clonidine
Surgery within 30 days of PCI
MACE events (MI, stent thrombosis, stroke) in perioperative period
Major bleeding requiring transfusion or reoperation
Signs of stent thrombosis (chest pain, ST-elevation)
Urgent need for anticoagulation reversal
Hemodynamic instability in anticoagulated patient
Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (glucose <250 mg/dL with pH <7.3, bicarbonate <18 mEq/L, elevated ketones)
Acute beta blocker withdrawal in patients on chronic therapy
Unexplained hemodynamic instability during surgery
Preoperative glucose ≥200 mg/dL
Acute severe hemodynamic instability with cardiopulmonary dysfunction
Unexplained severe hemodynamic instability persisting despite corrective therapy
STEMI after noncardiac surgery
Cardiogenic shock requiring mechanical circulatory support
Severe bleeding with hemodynamic compromise
extraction.follow_up: Close follow-up for patients with newly diagnosed atrial fibrillation to treat reversible causes and assess need for rhythm control and long-term anticoagulation. Myocardial injury after noncardiac surgery requires ongoing surveillance and management.
extraction.escalation_path: Team-based care for complex anatomy or unstable cardiovascular disease. Emergency focused cardiac ultrasound for unexplained hemodynamic instability when clinical expertise available. Subspecialty consultation for complex cardiovascular conditions during perioperative period.
